input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common .NET MAUI data binding issue. The XAML has `{Binding Title}` and `{Binding Description}`, but the labels are blank despite the ViewModel having values.

The most likely issue is that the `BindingContext` is not set. The XAML doesn't show a `BindingContext` being assigned, and the user hasn't mentioned setting it in code-behind either.

Another common issue is that the ViewModel doesn't implement `INotifyPropertyChanged`, but the user says "properties have values," which suggests the data is there but not showing — pointing more to a missing `BindingContext`.


I should provide a straightforward solution focusing on the most probable cause.
